classFeature.name: Favored Prey
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you have significant experience studying, tracking, hunting, and even talking to a certain type of enemy.

Choose a type of favored prey: beasts, constructs, dragonspawn, drakes, elementals, fey (grymkin), fiends (infernals), monstrosities, plants, trolls, or undead. Alternatively, you can select two races of humanoid (such as farrow and trollkin) as favored prey.

Once per turn, when you hit a favored prey with a weapon or spell attack, you can add a d4 to your damage roll. This die changes as you gain monster hunter levels, as shown in the Prey column of the Monster Hunter table.
Additionally, you may also add your prey die on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored prey, as well as on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.
When you gain this feature, you also learn one language of your choice that is spoken by your favored prey, if they speak one at all.

You choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language, at 6th, 9th, and 14th level. At these levels, you may also change an earlier favored prey choice, but you do not learn a new language or forget the older one.

classFeature.name: Weapon Expertise
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you have learned to apply your monster hunting skills when fighting with your preferred weaponry. When you have advantage on an attack roll against one of your favored enemies using a weapon you're proficient with, you can reroll one of the dice once.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again. You can choose an additional fighting style at 13th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 17th level in this class.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 6th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Survivalist
entries: At 6th level, your survival instincts have been sharpened by all the monsters you've hunted. When you roll a 9 or lower on an Intelligence (Investigation) check to investigate an area your favored enemies have recently been to or currently live in, or on a Wisdom (Survival) check to track your favored enemies, you can treat the d20 roll as a 10.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 9th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Grievous Wounds
entries: At 10th level, fighting monsters has given you a unique insight into their physiology, allowing you to see their weak points and understand how to inflict the most damage upon them. Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19–20 against your favored enemies. When you score a critical hit against one of your favored enemies, the target can't regain hit points until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Additional Fighting Style
entries: At 13th level, you can choose one additional fighting style from the Fighting Style feature options.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 14th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack (2)
entries: Beginning at 17th level, you can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: To the Ends of Caen
entries: At 18th level, you are tireless in pursuit of your prey. You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws, and you have advantage on saving throws when failure would result in gaining one or more levels of exhaustion.

classFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: At 20th level, attacks on your favored prey leave them rattled and unable to respond. When you hit your favored prey with a weapon attack, you can forgo adding your prey die to the damage roll to incapacitate your target. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Dexterity modifier) or be stunned until the end of your next turn. Doing this constitutes your use of the prey die for your turn. Additionally, you are immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

classFeature.name: Command Range
entries: At 1st level, your inspiring presence can steel your fellow warriors' nerves and even rally them before their panic becomes a full-blown rout. You have a command range for your warlord abilities. Your warlord level determines the distance of your command range, as shown in the Command Range column of the Warlord table.

classFeature.name: Battle Command
entries: Also at 1st level, you can use a bonus action to issue a command to a friendly creature you can see within your command range. The creature can use its reaction to make a weapon attack or to take the Dash, Disengage, or Help action on its turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Presence
entries: At 1st level, you can use a bonus action to choose a number of creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature) within your command range. Each target that can see or hear you gains temporary hit points equal to your warlord level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Tactical Strike
entries: At 2nd level, when you use your action to make a weapon attack against a creature you can see that is within 5 feet of a friendly creature you can see, you add your Charisma modifier to the damage roll.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Warlord's Orders
entries: Also at 5th level, you master the art of barking out orders in the chaos of battle. As an action, you can issue a command to nearby allies. Choose a number of friendly creatures up to your proficiency bonus that are within your command range and that can hear and understand you. Each chosen creature can use its reaction to move up to its speed or to make a weapon attack.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Leadership
entries: At 6th level, when you use your Inspiring Presence, affected creatures have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened} while they have temporary hit points granted by Inspiring Presence.

classFeature.name: Warlord's Tactics
entries: At 9th level, you can use a bonus action to choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. Until the end of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on attack rolls against the chosen creature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Overwhelming Force
entries: At 11th level, when you hit a creature with an attack on your turn, a friendly creature within your command range can use its reaction to make a melee weapon attack. If that attack hits, the target is knocked prone but doesn't take damage.

classFeature.name: Coordinated Assault
entries: At 13th level, when you use your Warlord's Orders, each affected creature that chooses to make a weapon attack deals an extra 1d8 weapon damage when it hits a target. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Master Tactician
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you can take an additional action on your turn. This action can be used only to take the Attack action or to use your Battle Command. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Supreme Commander
entries: When you reach 17th level, you become a master of leadership and tactics, and you can inspire your allies and dominate your enemies with ease.
You can use your Inspiring Presence a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

When you use your Tactical Strike, you can choose up to three creatures within your command range. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Unbreakable Resolve
entries: At 18th level, you and friendly creatures within your command range can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

classFeature.name: Battlefield Champion
entries: At 20th level, you embody raw power and command. Your Strength and Charisma scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

classFeature.name: Hunting Archetype
entries: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that defines how you hunt monsters. Choose Big-Game Hunter or Manhunter, both detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Hunting Archetype feature
entries: At 7th level, you gain a feature granted by your Hunting Archetype.

classFeature.name: Hunting Archetype feature
entries: At 11th level, you gain a feature granted by your Hunting Archetype.

classFeature.name: Hunting Archetype feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Hunting Archetype.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement

If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

entries: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that defines your leadership style and approach on the battlefield. The archetype you choose grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, and 14th level.

classFeature.name: Warlord Archetype feature

entries.name: Steady Aim
entries: At 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you learn to make stunning long-distance shots. If you forgo moving during your turn and make a ranged attack with a rifle at long range, attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage.

entries.name: Fire and Fade
entries: Also at 3rd level, you become adept at sniping enemies and disappearing into nearby cover before you can be spotted. When you hit a creature with ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to take the Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Run and Gun
entries: At 7th level, you have mastered the ability to use your ranged attacks to cover your next move. If you hit a creature with a ranged attack, you can move up to your speed as a bonus action. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: The Bigger They Are
entries: At 11th level, your knowledge of the hunt allows you to take down larger monsters more effectively. When you hit a creature that is at least one size larger than you, you can deal extra weapon damage equal to your proficiency bonus. (Double this bonus if you scored a critical hit.) You can use this feature three times. You regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Eye
entries: Starting at 15th level, you are adept at avoiding the teeth and claws of large beasts. Whenever a creature a size one size or more larger than you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on it. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attack roll. You may use this feature three times, and regain all uses after a short or long rest.

entries.name: Guerrilla Tactics
entries: At 3rd level, your ability to sneak away and hide from your enemies becomes uncanny. You can take a bonus action on each of your turns to take the Hide action.

entries.name: Ambush
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you're deadliest when you can surprise your foes. You may add your proficiency bonus to initiative rolls. Additionally, in the first round of combat you may add a roll of your prey die to damage against each creature you hit with a weapon attack who has not yet acted, even if that creature is not your favored enemy.

subclassFeature.name: Predatory Drive
entries: Starting at 7th level, while tracking, you and your group may move stealthily at a normal pace, and remain alert to danger while you do so. You also have advantage on saving throws to avoid exhaustion. If tracking your favored prey, you may also add your prey die to the roll. Additionally, you may remove a level of exhaustion after you take a short rest, and regain the ability to do so once you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Treewalker
entries: At 11th level, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.

subclassFeature.name: Master Tracker
entries: At 15th level, your senses are attuned to tracking prey and are equal to those of the most cunning predators. When you succeed on a Wisdom (Survival) check to locate your favored enemies, you know their location if it is within 100 feet.

entries.name: Intimidating Presence
entries: At 3rd level, when you use your Inspiring Presence, you also frighten nearby enemies. A hostile creature of your choice within 5 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. Attacks against the frightened creature deal extra weapon damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Brutal Tactics
entries: Beginning at 7th level, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone, and you can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks from the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Conqueror's Presence
entries: At 10th level, you can use your action to gain temporary hit points equal to your warlord level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). In addition, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on weapon attack rolls until the end of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodthirsty Orders
entries: At 14th level, when you use your Battle Command, choose a friendly creature within your command range. That creature can immediately take the Attack action, and its next attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

entries.name: Defensive Maneuvers
entries: At 3rd level, when you use your Battle Command, you can choose one creature within your command range. The target has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Sentinel's Stance
entries: At 7th level, you can use your action to enter a sentinel's stance. While in this stance, you have advantage on opportunity attacks, and creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you even if they take the Disengage action before leaving your reach. Additionally, you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack against a creature that attacks a friendly creature within 5 feet of you, provided that you can see the attacker.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian's Eye
entries: When you reach 10th level, you learn how to keep an eye on everyone under your command. When a friendly creature within your command range makes a saving throw, you can use your reaction to shout a warning. If the creature can hear you, it has advantage on the saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Indomitable Will
entries: At 14th level, while you are conscious, friendly creatures within your command range can't be {@condition frightened} or {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Tactical Analysis
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use an action to choose a creature that you can see within your command range. You learn the creature's current hit points; any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities it has; and any conditions affecting it. Additionally, the next time you or a friendly creature attacks the target creature before the end of your next turn, the attacker has advantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Controlled Advance
entries: At 7th level, you can use your action to direct your allies' advance with precision. Each friendly creature within your command range can move up to its speed and has resistance to all damage from opportunity attacks. Additionally, until the end of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on attack rolls made against any creature that made an opportunity attack against a friendly creature during this movement. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Strategist
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you roll initiative, you can take a bonus action to grant yourself and friendly creatures within your command range a bonus to their initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Additionally, when you use your Inspiring Presence, each affected creature gains a bonus to attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier while it has temporary hit points granted by Inspiring Presence.

subclassFeature.name: Warlord's Gambit
entries: At 14th level, when you use your Tactical Strike, you can choose to forgo the damage bonus and instead force the target to make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the target is {@condition stunned} until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Field Surgery
entries: Starting at 3rd level when you choose this specialization, you know the anatomy and physiology of living creatures just as well as you know the properties of chemicals and reagents. You gain proficiency with your choice of either a forensic kit or a poisoner's kit, and you double your proficiency bonus when you make Wisdom (Medicine) checks. You also gain a free clockwork injector if you don't already have one.

entries.name: New Fast Brew: Simple Stimulant
entries: You mix ingredients to create a dose of simple stimulant—a bitter, milky fluid that provides a surge of energy and alertness. As an action, a creature can consume a dose of simple stimulant to ignore the highest level of exhaustion it currently suffers. This effect lasts for 1 hour. In addition, a creature can consume a dose of simple stimulant during a short rest to reduce its exhaustion level by 1.

entries.name: This May Sting a Little
entries: At 3rd level, you become adept at using clockwork injectors to efficiently deliver alchemical infusions. As a bonus action, you can make a Wisdom (Medicine) check against a creature that is not a construct or an undead, contested by the creature's Charisma (Deception) check. If you succeed, you have advantage on attack rolls against that creature with your clockwork injector until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Medicinal Properties
entries: At 6th level, you have learned how to work quickly and efficiently by piggybacking multiple effects on a single dose. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose one of the following additional effects. These effects don't affect constructs or undead.

entries.name: Adrenaline
entries: Until the start of your next turn, the creature rolls one additional weapon damage die when it hits a target with a melee attack. When this effect wears off, the creature takes 1d10 poison damage.

entries.name: Anesthesia
entries: The creature gains temporary hit points equal to your alchemist level. These temporary hit points last for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Deliriant
entries: The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of all other creatures for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Inhibitor
entries: The creature must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or be unable to cast spells until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Revivifier
entries: The creature regains a number of hit points equal to one roll of its Hit Dice. This effect doesn't expend any of the creature's Hit Dice.

entries.name: Sedative
entries: The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Stabilizer
entries: The creature can end either one disease or one condition afflicting it. The condition can be blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, or stunned. In addition, the creature is immune to the chosen condition until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Animal Magnetism
entries: At 10th level, the often unconventional nature of your experiments forces you to ply your trade on the fringes of civilization, where you must regularly experiment on the beasts of the field. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose to apply the effects of this feature instead of one of the effects provided by your Medicinal Properties.

A creature affected by your Animal Magnetism must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for a number of hours equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). While charmed in this way, the creature treats you as a friend and obeys any verbal commands that you issue to it (no action required by you), as long as it can understand you. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each hour, or immediately if you or one of your companions harms it. This feature has no effect on constructs or undead.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Adrenalize
entries: Starting at 14th level, you learn how to add a quick jolt to your alchemical concoctions that can help 'juice up' beasts and soldiers on the battlefield. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose two additional effects from your Medicinal Properties, rather than just one. You can use your Animal Magnetism to replace one of these effects.

subclassFeature.name: Life Is Pain
entries: At 18th level, you embrace the unpredictability that comes with experimenting directly on living subjects and are quite comfortable ignoring their safety in order to conduct your unorthodox and sometimes unethical experiments. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose to increase or decrease one of that creature's ability scores by an amount equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) for 1 minute. After this time, the creature's ability score returns to normal, but the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d10 poison damage and become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills, as well as proficiency with a trapper's kit. In addition, you can use your Mechanik's Savvy so long as you are outdoors and not on or inside a structure or vehicle or underground. A trapper's kit can be used in place of thieves' tools to disable a nonmagical trap.

entries.name: Set a Trap
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you can use your action and expend one use of your Tinkering to set a trap anywhere within 5 feet of you. The DC to spot, detect, or disable one of your traps equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier. When you set a trap, you can expend a second use of your Tinkering to roll a Tinkering die and add its total to the DC. If you are setting a premade trap instead, you can set it as a bonus action. Choose one of the following trap types when you set a trap: Disabling Trap (Dex save or Tinkering die damage + exhaustion), Immobilizing Trap (Dex save or {@condition restrained}), or Lethal Trap (Dex save or 3× Tinkering die + proficiency bonus damage, half on success).

subclassFeature.name: Cautious Advance
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Skilled Trapper
entries: When you reach 10th level, you can use your Set a Trap as a bonus action, rather than as an action. In addition, choose one of the following trap styles: Brutal Trap (add Intelligence modifier to damage), Hidden Trap (double proficiency bonus for detection/disarm DC), or Sturdy Trap (immobilizing trap escape DC +5).

subclassFeature.name: Trapper's Instinct
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you have a sixth sense for avoiding traps. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to detect traps and secret doors. You also have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps, as well as resistance to any damage dealt by traps.

subclassFeature.name: Master Trapper
entries: At 18th level, you are a master at setting traps. Choose a second trap style, from those in Skilled Trapper or from: Inescapable Trap (disadvantage on Dex saves to avoid trap effects), Reliable Trap (trap resets if target succeeds on save), or Spiteful Trap (secondary trap triggers when primary is disarmed). You can apply either or both styles to any trap you set. At the end of a long rest, you can replace any trap style you know with another.

entries.name: Rage of the Devourer
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you enter a rage, you can choose to channel the power of the Devourer Wurm. While in this state, your teeth and claws become razor-sharp, allowing you to make unarmed strikes that deal slashing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier. Whenever you kill a creature that is not a construct or an undead while in this state, you can use your bonus action to devour their still-beating heart, granting you temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level + your Constitution modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Savage Feast
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you use your bonus action to devour a still-beating heart, you can choose to share the feast with an ally within 5 feet of you. Doing so grants that ally temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level.

subclassFeature.name: Chieftain's Presence
entries: Starting at 10th level, you gain a commanding presence that inspires fear in your enemies and gives strength to your allies. While you are raging, your allies within 30 feet of you gain a bonus to their damage rolls equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1).

subclassFeature.name: Beastly Frenzy
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you enter a rage, you can choose to fully embrace the power of the Devourer Wurm. While in this state, your movement speed increases by 10 feet, your unarmed strikes deal 1d8 extra weapon damage, and whenever you score a critical hit with an unarmed strike or a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to make another attack with advantage.

entries.name: Armored Training
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this path, you learn how to channel your natural fury even while covered in heavy plate. You gain proficiency with heavy armor and can gain the benefits of your Rage and Fast Movement features even while wearing heavy armor.

entries.name: Brutalize
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn how to deal crippling blows that leave your opponents struggling to respond. When you hit a creature with a melee attack while raging, you can choose to impose disadvantage on that creature's next attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Thresher
entries: By 6th level, your training with long, chopping polearms has made you as adept at fighting crowds as you are at taking down large prey. When you use your Reckless Attack, you can use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within your reach, with a separate attack roll for each target. If you do so, you don't have advantage on your attack rolls. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Finisher
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can channel your ferocity to take advantage of blows struck by your allies, allowing you to bring down even the mightiest enemies with a flurry of bloody strikes. While raging, you have advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that has less than half of its hit point maximum.

subclassFeature.name: Take Down
entries: By 14th level, you've mastered the art of inflicting wounds that wear down your enemies rather than fell them in a single strike. When you hit a creature with a melee attack while raging, you can choose to use this feature as a reaction. If you do so, that creature can't regain hit points for a number of minutes equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you join the College of Fauna at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Animal Handling skill if you aren't already proficient in it.

entries.name: Idyllic Recital
entries: At 3rd level, you have the power to tame beasts in addition to humanoids and other sentient beings. As an action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to entrance beasts. Each beast within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. If you or creatures friendly to you are fighting a beast, it has advantage on the saving throw. On a failed save, the beast is {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

entries.name: Voice of Nature
entries: Also at 3rd level, beasts can understand your speech. Even though most beasts lack the intelligence to understand more than basic concepts, you can use straightforward gestures to convey simple requests or present questions to friendly beasts.

subclassFeature.name: Pack Bond
entries: By 6th level, your connection to animals is strong enough that you can forge bonds beyond simple companionship. Bonding with a friendly beast requires a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. You can bond with a number of beasts up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one beast), but their combined challenge rating can't exceed your proficiency bonus. A bonded beast fights alongside you until you or your companions do anything harmful to it, and it acts on its own initiative in combat. A bonded beast gains the Bondmate special trait: it can reroll an attack roll of 1 or 2 against a target creature if the bonded character is within 5 feet of the target.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Refrain
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can reach beyond a being's senses and speak to its primal nature. As an action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to entrance one creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage. If the target has an Intelligence of 4 or lower, it has disadvantage on the saving throw. Alternatively, as an action you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to instill fear in an audience: each creature within 60 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute, taking 1d4 psychic damage at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Warder's Discipline
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain access to a special pool of dice called defense dice, which are d6s. The number of defense dice in your pool equals your proficiency bonus. A defense die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended defense dice when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Warder's Tactics
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to use your defense dice to hinder your enemies' attacks. When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the attack roll by the number you roll on your defense die. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the GM determines whether the attack hits.

subclassFeature.name: Roadblock
entries: Starting at 7th level, when a creature within 5 feet of you hits a friendly creature with an attack, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your defense die + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial melee weapon to use this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Warder's Stance
entries: At 10th level, you can use a bonus action to enter a defensive stance. When you do so, your speed is halved and you gain the following benefits until you're incapacitated or end the stance as a bonus action: Enhanced Tactics (Warder's Tactics reduces the attack roll by defense die + proficiency bonus), Parry (Roadblock can expend up to proficiency bonus defense dice), and Superior Defense (Dodge action lets you expend one defense die to also take Dash or Disengage as a bonus action). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian's Strike
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can unleash a powerful strike against a creature that tries to harm your allies. When a creature within 5 feet of you attacks a friendly creature or targets it with a spell, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to make a weapon attack against the attacking creature. If the attack hits, add the defense die to the attack's damage roll. You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial melee weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Warder's Aura
entries: At 18th level, as an action you can create a 30-foot-radius sphere that lasts for 1 minute, centered on you and moving with you. For the duration, when an affected friendly creature takes damage, makes a saving throw, or makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the damage by or add to the roll the number you roll + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Wilderness Warrior
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Perception and Survival skills, and moving through nonmagical difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.

entries.name: Pack of Wolves
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to coordinate attacks with your allies. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use a bonus action to allow a friendly creature within 5 feet of you to make a melee attack against the same target without using its reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Power Swell
entries: At 7th level, a wild vitality rushes through you. As a bonus action, you can channel this surge through your allies as well. Until the start of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within 5 feet of you roll one additional weapon damage die when you hit a target with a melee attack. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hungry like the Wolf
entries: At 10th level, when you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can move up to half your speed immediately after the attack. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Wolf's Senses
entries: At 15th level, your connection to your predatory nature sharpens your senses to a razor's edge. You have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. (If you naturally have darkvision without this feature, the range of your darkvision increases by 60 feet.) Additionally, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell or hearing.

subclassFeature.name: Beastly Fury
entries: At 18th level, when a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. If the attack hits, you can roll one additional weapon damage die.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this tradition, you gain proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills.

entries.name: Crawl
entries: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you become as deadly on the ground as you are on your feet. You can't be knocked prone unless you allow it, and being prone doesn't impose disadvantage on your melee attack rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Impervious Flesh
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you channel your inner predator to make yourself impervious to the attacks of your enemies. When you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier. In addition, when you use your Patient Defense, you can spend 1 additional ki point to have resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Grab and Toss
entries: At 6th level, you can fling away your enemies like a wild gator tossing an attacker. When you are grappling a creature, you can use your action to throw it in a straight line up to a number of feet equal to five times your Strength modifier. A creature thrown in this manner takes damage equal to your Martial Arts die and must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone. If a thrown creature strikes a solid surface or another creature, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Martial Arts die for every 10 feet it was thrown. A creature struck by the thrown creature must succeed on a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked prone.

subclassFeature.name: Patient Predator
entries: By 11th level, you have learned how to wait for the perfect moment to strike, just like the great beasts of the swamp. You have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that has already taken its action this turn.

subclassFeature.name: Gyre
entries: At 17th level, you learn how to mimic the death roll of the great gators of the swamp. You have advantage on checks made to initiate or maintain a grapple. A creature grappled by you is also {@condition restrained}. While a creature is grappled by you, you can spend any number of ki points as a bonus action to enter a death roll. For each ki point you spend, the creature takes an amount of damage equal to your Martial Arts die + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Tenets of the Chosen
entries: By Any Means Necessary: Nothing will stop you from ensuring that no harm comes to those you protect. Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you. Protection: You are the sanctuary of the innocent, and behind your shield, they can find respite.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at certain paladin levels: 3rd (guiding bolt, thunderous smite), 5th (grave wind, hand of fate), 9th (beacon of hope, mirage), 13th (lamentation, stranglehold), 17th (axiomatic spear, wrath of Dhunia).

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Divine Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can use your Channel Divinity to shield your allies. For 1 minute, each friendly creature within 15 feet of you gains a bonus to AC equal to your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Righteous Vengeance
entries: When a friendly creature within 30 feet of you is damaged by a hostile creature's attack, you can use your reaction to respond with holy indignation. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with melee weapons for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Defense
entries: Starting at 7th level, as a bonus action you can choose one friendly creature within 10 feet of you. You and the chosen creature gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (your choice) from nonmagical attacks for 1 minute or until you're no longer within 10 feet of each other. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Protector
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can deflect blows directed at your allies. When a friendly creature within 5 feet of you is hit by a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack roll against the attacker. If your total equals or exceeds the attacker's total, the attacker's attack is deflected and automatically misses. If you are wielding a shield, you have advantage on the attack roll. If you are wielding a heavy reach weapon and successfully deflect, you can immediately make an opportunity attack against the attacker as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Defense (30 ft.)
entries: At 18th level, the range of your Aura of Defense increases to 30 feet. Additionally, you no longer need to choose a damage type — you and the chosen creature gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Final Vengeance
entries: At 20th level, your divine purpose fuels your blows. When a friendly creature that you can see is hit by a hostile creature, you can use your reaction to move up to your speed toward the hostile creature and make one attack against it. Any hit you score against the creature counts as a critical hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Tenets of the Paragon
entries: Be the Light: Be a glorious beacon for all who live in despair. Let the light of your joy and courage shine forth in all your deeds. Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you. Honor: Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example to them.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at certain paladin levels: 3rd (elusive, shield), 5th (flashing blade, sacrosanct, spellpiercer), 9th (beacon of hope, blade of radiance), 13th (freedom of movement, stoneskin), 17th (axiomatic spear, purification).

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Inspiring Presence
entries: As a bonus action, you can inspire others through your very presence. Choose a number of friendly creatures equal to your proficiency bonus. Each creature gains one Inspiration die, a d6 (becomes d8 at 7th level). Once within the next 10 minutes, a creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Sacred Weapon
entries: As an action, you can imbue one weapon you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light 20 feet beyond that.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon's Example
entries: Starting at 7th level, choose one of the following ideals. When you use this feature, choose a number of friendly creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum one creature) within 10 feet of you. For the next 10 minutes, you and the chosen creatures gain the benefits described below.

entries.name: Bravery
entries: Affected creatures can't be {@condition charmed}. If already charmed, the condition ends.

entries.name: Strength
entries: Affected creatures double their Strength modifier on attack damage rolls and have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Wisdom
entries: Affected creatures double their Wisdom modifier (unless negative) on Wisdom checks and have advantage on Wisdom saving throws.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Retaliatory Thrust
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to move up to 10 feet toward that creature and then make one melee weapon attack against it. If the attack hits, the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon's Example (30 ft.)
entries: At 18th level, the range of your Paragon's Example increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Mastered Strike
entries: At 20th level, divine fury guides your strikes. You can choose to use this feature at the beginning of a combat encounter, when everyone rolls initiative. If you do, during the first round of combat your first weapon attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 18–20, and if you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to force the target to lose all its damage resistances until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature three times. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Mount Mastery
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with Animal Handling and gain a Skirovik mountain goat (a giant goat) as your mount. While riding your mount, you have advantage on initiative rolls, and your mount gains: speed +10 ft., can move through difficult terrain without penalty, and can make a Ram attack as a bonus action dealing 1d8 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Elemental Shroud
entries: At 3rd level, as a bonus action, you can create a shroud of fog or sheets of rain that grants you advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide. While shrouded, creatures have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to see you. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once). You regain all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Lightning Strike
entries: At 7th level, you gain the ability to strike your opponents with flashes of lightning. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can choose to deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage to the target. Additionally, if the target is wearing metal armor or carrying a metal shield, it takes an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

subclassFeature.name: Fog Step
entries: At 11th level, as a bonus action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that is obscured by fog, mist, rain, or natural darkness. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once). You regain all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Storm's Fury
entries: At 15th level, as an action, you can create a 20-foot-radius sphere of swirling winds and driving rain centered on yourself. Each creature within the sphere must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or take 4d8 bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't knocked prone. You can use this ability once per long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Stealth skill. If you are already proficient in this skill, double your proficiency bonus when you make an ability check with the skill.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Work
entries: At 7th level, you have perfected the ability to deftly switch from melee attacks to ranged attacks. When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to make a single ranged attack against another creature.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Critical
entries: At 11th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

subclassFeature.name: Snap Fire
entries: At 11th level, your mastery of the double crossbow allows you to fire an unnatural number of bolts in a spectacular display of trigger discipline. In the first round of a combat, you can treat your double crossbow as if it were affected by the endless magazine spell and can ignore the loading property of a dual crossbow. You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sprint
entries: At 15th level, if you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you can use a bonus action to move up to your speed without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this feature three times. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Soothing Song
entries: At 3rd level, your native ability to nurture warbeasts allows you to put them at ease and make them more compliant. When a friendly warlock makes a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check to keep a warbeast under control and you are within the warlock's control range, you can use your reaction to aid the warlock's efforts, giving the warlock advantage on the ability check.

subclassFeature.name: Resonance Extension
entries: At 7th level, if you are within a friendly warlock's control range, you can use a bonus action to increase the warlock's control range by 10 feet × your proficiency bonus. This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Warbeast Conditioning
entries: At 11th level, as a bonus action, you can touch a friendly warbeast and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. If the check succeeds, you can use one of the following options: Animus Adept (cast the warbeast's animus once without using an action or bonus action), Calming Touch (remove 1 fury point from the warbeast), or Healing Caress (cast cure wounds as a 1st-level spell on the warbeast without using a spell slot). You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Furious Attacker
entries: At 15th level, as a bonus action, you can touch a friendly warbeast and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. If the check succeeds, you can remove 1 fury point from the warbeast to use one of the following abilities: Attack Boost (advantage on your next attack roll), Damage Boost (next hit deals extra 1d8 damage), or Shake It Off (immediately remake a saving throw against an ongoing effect, with advantage). You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Intimidation, or Survival.

entries.name: Mounted Marauder
entries: At 3rd level, your command of your mount makes it your most reliable accomplice. When you use your Cunning Action, you can mount a creature that is within 5 feet of you or dismount as part of your bonus action, and doing so doesn't cost you any movement or provoke opportunity attacks.

entries.name: Ambush Attack
entries: Starting at 3rd level, if you take the Attack action while mounted and your steed moves at least 20 feet in a straight line before the attack, you can use your Sneak Attack against the target even if you don't have advantage on the attack roll, but not if you have disadvantage on it.

subclassFeature.name: Unwelcome Guest
entries: At 9th level, you master the art of getting away. When you or your mount are attacked while mounted, you can use your reaction immediately after the attack to force your mount to move up to half its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. In addition, if you are unmounted and your steed moves within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to mount your steed as it passes by.

subclassFeature.name: Sidesaddle Slasher
entries: At 13th level, your skill as a rider allows you to use your mount's mass to extend your attack range. On your turn, when you make an attack with a one-handed melee weapon while mounted, you add 5 feet to your reach.

subclassFeature.name: Blood on the Saddle
entries: Beginning at 17th level, your mount's bond with you extends all the way to death's door. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while mounted but not killed outright, you aren't dismounted unless the triggering effect says otherwise. If you fail a death saving throw while mounted, you can reroll it with advantage (once per long rest). After you make a death saving throw while mounted, you can issue your mount a brief command.

entries.name: Poacher's Instincts
entries: Starting at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain proficiency in the Deception and Survival skills.

entries.name: Trap Ambush
entries: Also at 3rd level, your cunningly placed traps can deal devastating damage. When a creature triggers one of your traps, you can add your Sneak Attack damage to the damage dealt by the trap.

entries.name: Trapmaker
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to craft simple traps and snares out of natural materials found in the wilderness. You can spend 10 minutes constructing a trap that is equivalent to a hunting trap. The trap remains active for 24 hours or until triggered.

subclassFeature.name: Camouflage
entries: At 9th level, you gain the ability to blend in with your surroundings. When you are in an environment that contains natural elements such as rocks, trees, or grass, you can use your action to camouflage yourself. While camouflaged, you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to remain hidden. The camouflage lasts for 1 hour or until you take the Dash action or are hit by an attack or spell.

subclassFeature.name: Acute Senses
entries: At 13th level, your senses are sharp enough that you can perceive the world around you with great clarity. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to detect hidden creatures or objects, and you can see in dim light as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light.

subclassFeature.name: Master Hunter
entries: At 17th level, you are a master of the hunt. The time you must spend picking up a creature's trail is halved. You can use your action to mark a creature you are tracking, giving you advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track it and attack rolls against it for 1 hour or until it is captured or killed.

entries.name: Arcanivore Magic
entries: Starting when you choose this origin at 1st level, you learn additional spells as you level: 1st (faerie fire, find familiar), 3rd (arcanist's magic aura, locate object), 5th (glyph of warding, nondetection), 7th (arcane eye, locate creature), 9th (arcane hand, legend lore). Each counts as a sorcerer spell but doesn't count against your spells known.

entries.name: Hedge Ward
entries: Also starting at 1st level, you have advantage on saving throws against cantrips. Additionally, at 2nd level, when another creature within 5 feet of you casts a spell of 1st level or higher, or when another creature targets you with a spell other than a cantrip, you can use your reaction to regain a number of sorcery points equal to the level of the spell cast.

subclassFeature.name: Energy Store
entries: At 6th level, your innate ability to manipulate magical forces is strong enough that you can touch the essence of those close to you. As part of a long rest, you can channel unexpended sorcery points into a willing living creature (typically a familiar). You can't channel more than half your maximum sorcery points (rounded up) in this way. As a bonus action, you can touch the creature and draw sorcery points from the pool stored inside it.

subclassFeature.name: Devour Spell
entries: At 14th level, when a spell of 5th level or lower is cast within 5 feet of you, or if you are the only target of a spell of 5th level or lower, you can use your reaction to absorb the spell harmlessly into yourself, completely negating it. In addition, until you finish a long rest, you can use sorcery points (as shown in the Creating Spell Slots table) to cast that spell but can't cast the spell by expending spell slots. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Disenchanting Grasp
entries: At 18th level, as an action, you can regain hit points by draining the enchantment from an item you're holding or that is held by a creature you have successfully grappled. The magic item makes a saving throw against your spell save DC (with a bonus based on rarity: Common +1, Uncommon +2, Rare +3, Very Rare +4, Legendary +5, Unique +6). On a failed save, the item loses all magical properties for 1d4 rounds and you regain hit points (1d6 per rarity tier). An item drained for three consecutive turns permanently loses all its magical properties. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Twin Magic
entries: At 1st level, you learn additional spells as you level: 1st (cold-blooded, swamp pit), 3rd (acid arrow, bilious fog), 5th (bestow curse, conjure animals), 7th (control water, giant insect), 9th (black tide, contagion). Each counts as a sorcerer spell but doesn't count against your spells known.

entries.name: Lil' Buddy
entries: Also at 1st level, you have a conjoined twin with whom you share a spiritual link. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on saving throws against being {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition stunned}, and knocked {@condition unconscious}.

subclassFeature.name: Share the Load
entries: At 6th level, your twin helps you cast spells. When you cast a cantrip and use your Twinned Spell to target a second creature, doing so doesn't cost you any sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Two Heads Are Better than One
entries: At 14th level, your twin can take over the focus of spells you cast together. You can now concentrate on two spells at once.

subclassFeature.name: Double Down
entries: Upon reaching 18th level, your twinned soul grants you enhanced mastery over your sorcerous magic. When you spend sorcery points, roll a d20. On a roll of 11 or higher, you immediately gain 1 sorcery point.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Maw lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell: 1st (purify food and drink, inflict wounds), 2nd (darkvision, locate animals or plants), 3rd (nondetection, rift), 4th (compulsion, locate creature), 5th (maelstrom, wall of force).

entries.name: Multivore
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can gain sustenance from anything you ingest. As an action, you can eat any solid matter to gain temporary hit points equal to 1d4 + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). This strengthens your teeth and jaw, allowing you to chew and ingest wood and leather without difficulty. You can gain temporary hit points from this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Predatory Awareness
entries: At 6th level, the growing hunger inside of you heightens your senses. You gain proficiency in your choice of two of the following skills: Investigation, Perception, or Survival. Alternatively, you gain proficiency in one of those skills and double your proficiency bonus on checks made with that skill. In addition, your constant awareness means you can't be surprised, and you gain blindsight out to a range of 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Cannibal Spirit
entries: At 10th level, your connection to the Maw is so strong that the supernatural hunger inside you begins feeding on your body, leaving lean muscle behind. Your Strength or Dexterity score and ability score maximum increase by 1, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you have advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) rolls against Medium or smaller creatures.

subclassFeature.name: The Hungering Void
entries: Beginning at 14th level, as an action, you can become a gateway into the hungry abyss until the start of your next turn. You can't take any additional actions, bonus actions, or reactions on the turn you use this feature, and you can't maintain concentration spells. Beginning with the creature closest to you, each creature within 20 feet of you must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature within 10 feet of you has disadvantage on the saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is pulled 10 feet toward you (20 feet if it fails by 10 or more). On a successful save, it isn't pulled.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at certain shaman levels: 1st (command, false life), 3rd (darkness, stir the dead), 5th (death pact, ichor of Kossk), 7th (devour spirit, phantasmal killer), 9th (antilife shell, raise dead).

entries.name: Cold-Blooded
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you become somewhat disconnected from the world of the living. You have advantage on Charisma saving throws against being {@condition charmed}.

entries.name: Grave Secrets
entries: Also at 1st level, you gain proficiency in your choice of the Arcana skill or the Intimidation skill, and you have advantage on ability checks made with the chosen skill.

entries.name: Resist the Call
entries: At 1st level, as an action, you can chant an exhortation that helps your companions stave off death. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you has advantage on death saving throws until the end of your next turn. In addition, if the creature is frightened, paralyzed, petrified, or stunned, the condition ends. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites: Grave Charms
entries: At 2nd level, you learn a new rite. Choose either Terminal Whispers or Unhallow.

entries.name: Terminal Whispers
entries: When a living creature dies within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to draw upon its spirit energy. If you do so, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Unhallow
entries: As an action, you call upon the spirits in your area to curse and vex your foes. For 1 minute, each hostile creature within 30 feet of you has disadvantage on concentration rolls to maintain spells, and each friendly creature within 30 feet of you has advantage on saving throws to avoid being affected by spells cast by hostile creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Bone Shaker
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can channel the power of the grave to take control of the flesh and bones of living creatures. When you deal damage to a creature other than a construct, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you can use your reaction to take temporary control of the creature and can immediately force it to move up to its speed and make a single melee attack, after which your control ends. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. Beginning at 14th level, you can use this feature twice between rests.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Goods
entries: Beginning at 6th level, your melee attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Rise
entries: At 8th level, you can use an action to reanimate a humanoid within 5 feet of you that has died within the last hour. To determine the creature's hit points, roll a number of Hit Dice equal to your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier (minimum one Hit Die). Once you do so, you must finish a long rest before you can use this feature again (except as noted in Refuse the Call). The reanimated creature becomes undead with Turn Resistance and Unliving Flesh, but automatically fails death saving throws and can no longer be raised if it dies in this state. At the end of each long rest, you must sacrifice spell slots (combined level ≥ 1d4 per creature) to maintain each reanimated creature.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Aspect
entries: At 17th level, you are suffused with the energies of the grave. You are immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition and have immunity to necrotic damage and poison damage.

subclassFeature.name: Refuse the Call
entries: Upon reaching 17th level, whenever you start your turn with 0 hit points and would normally make your first death saving throw, you can use your Rise on yourself if you have not already used it since your last long rest. The effects take place immediately. While you are reanimated in this way, you can't use your Rise on yourself or another creature but can still sacrifice spell slots to maintain reanimated creatures.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at certain shaman levels: 1st (create or destroy water, pestilent wind), 3rd (darkness, pass without trace), 5th (bestow curse, glimpse of Sike Dulra), 7th (control water, divination), 9th (Ashiga's breath, mislead).

entries.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you learn one cantrip of your choice from the following list: acid splash, friends, guidance, or minor illusion. This cantrip counts as a shaman cantrip for you but doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

entries.name: Influence
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose one creature within 5 feet of you. Until the end of your next turn, that creature has advantage on its next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites: Dreams of Ashiga
entries: At 2nd level, you learn a new rite. Choose either Call the Mists or Hazy Portents.

entries.name: Call the Mists
entries: As an action, you create a 60-foot-radius sphere of fog that lasts for a number of rounds equal to half your shaman level (rounded up), moving with you. Choose a number of creatures up to your Wisdom modifier. You and the chosen creatures gain a bonus to AC equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) against attacks made by creatures more than 5 feet away. You and the chosen creatures can see through the fog clearly, but its area is heavily obscured for all other creatures.

entries.name: Hazy Portents
entries: As a reaction, you can force one creature within 30 feet of you to reroll an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, but the creature must use the new roll.

subclassFeature.name: Protection of the Mists
entries: By 6th level, you have mastered the art of using your trade to protect yourself and your allies. When you gain a bonus to AC from your Ritual Protections, you can choose a number of friendly creatures up to your Wisdom modifier (minimum one creature). Until the start of your next turn, each chosen creature gains the same bonus to AC if it is within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Mirage
entries: Beginning at 8th level, you become an expert at being in one place while appearing to be in another. When you are hit by an attack, you can choose to ignore the effects of that attack. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Waters
entries: Beginning at 17th level, as a bonus action, you can expend a spell slot to call up dark water from the earth, turning the land around you into marsh in a 30-foot aura. The aura lasts for a number of rounds equal to the level of the spell slot expended and moves with you. For the duration, the area is difficult terrain for hostile creatures, and each friendly creature in the area (including you) has half cover against attacks from within 5 feet and three-quarters cover against attacks from more than 5 feet away.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain resonance spells at certain warlock levels: 1st (mud foot), 3rd (dirge of mists), 5th (acid arrow), 10th (death pact), 13th (devour spirit), 17th (absorb flesh).

entries.name: Bestial Connection
entries: At 1st level, animal friendship and speak with animals are added to your known spells but can't be replaced when you gain a level. You can cast animal friendship, speak with animals, and animal messenger without spending fury points, but you can only target blackhides, boneswarms, cane leeches, ironbacks, snappers, swamp horrors, and tatzylwurms with these spells. At 3rd level, animal messenger is added to your known spells. Additionally, you can bond to one swamp warbeast without counting the bond against the number of items you can attune to.

entries.name: Patient Hunter
entries: Starting at 1st level, when a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range makes an opportunity attack, you can use your reaction to give that warbeast advantage on the attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Flesh of the Fallen
entries: At 6th level, when you or a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range reduces a hostile creature other than a construct or an undead to 0 hit points, you can use a bonus action to consume some of the creature's flesh or have the warbeast do so. Consuming the flesh regains 1d8 hit points for each point of the creature's Constitution modifier (minimum 1d8 hit points).

subclassFeature.name: Death Roll
entries: Starting at 10th level, when a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range reduces a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to force the warbeast to move up to 15 feet and then make one melee attack against a hostile creature within its reach. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Cold-Blooded Cunning
entries: Starting at 14th level, as a bonus action, you can spend 1 fury point to give each warbeast that is under your control and in your control range advantage on saving throws to avoid being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Reptilian Hide
entries: At 18th level, as a bonus action, you can spend 2 fury points to give each warbeast that is under your control and in your control range resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Monster Hunter
entries: Bursting through the jungle canopy, a satyr charges headlong toward its prey: an Ordic hunter standing in the middle of a small clearing. Steadfast and deliberate, the hunter steadies her aim and pulls the trigger of her modified Vanar Liberator, puncturing a hole through the eye socket of the oncoming beast. The satyr's momentum carries it onward until it falls flat in front of the hunter. The totem hunter has the gorax's scent now, and nothing will keep him from finding his quarry and adding its carcass to his stockpile of trophies. Moving swiftly and silently through the forest, the totem hunter surprises the creature and jams his sharpened kelkax through its abdomen, severing its spine and killing it instantly. The long-bearded Kossite peers out from behind a web of black pine needles heavy with snow as his quarry comes into view in the clearing below. The infernalist had sought to escape punishment by fleeing the city, but the Kossite is here to deliver retribution, if not justice. Without a sound, he drops down from his perch in the tree and ends the traitor's life with a quick hack of his axe. These individuals are all monster hunters. Whether motivated by amassing trophies, collecting rewards, or simply hunting dangerous prey for its own sake, they are committed to taking on the deadliest prey in the wilds and coming out on top. Dealing with such creatures requires a particular set of skills, and several traditions have sprung up across western Immoren, each with its own specialized methods for tracking prey and eliminating it.

entries.name: The Most Dangerous Prey
entries: Monster hunters may seem like any other mercenary with a gun or sword, but their unique skills set them apart from other would-be adventurers and military recruits. These individuals can track their quarry with ease and know the exact weapon, spell, or tool needed to end their prey's existence. They can make the largest apex predator hesitate before attacking and can even force these creatures to fear for their lives. Patient, astute, and quick to act, monster hunters ply their trades far and wide. There's no terrain they can't handle and no creature they won't hunt.

entries.name: Wild Things
entries: Monster hunters are often found traveling the farthest reaches of Immoren, whether hunting and tracking prey or training others in the trade. The most arduous and inhospitable lands are their home, as such areas are fertile hunting grounds—a whetstone for sharpening a hunter's skills. Beasts of all sizes and shapes become their targets, and nothing less than death will stop them from executing their charge.

Although there's no official guild or lodge for monster hunters, hunters will typically welcome one who walks the same path unless the newcomer is there to poach a kill. Honor among hunters exists, but it lasts only until one of them is forced into the role of prey—a role against which a hunter naturally rebels. There is much to be learned from different monster hunters, as their skills differ in accordance with their preferred quarry and weaponry. Some find guns and rifles more suitable, while others prefer to get close in order to hew a beast to pieces with axe or sword.

entries.name: Warlord
entries: A human captain in plate armor leads her troops into battle, barking out orders and directing the flow of the fight. She wields a greatsword with deadly precision, cutting through enemy lines and inspiring her allies with her bravery. Using her tactical prowess to position her allies for maximum effectiveness, the captain leads a well-coordinated assault that proves crucial to her army's victory. A trollkin chieftain charges into the fray, wielding a massive greataxe with devastating power. He is flanked by a pair of pyg fighters, who dart in and out of the battle as they take down enemies with their shortswords. The chieftain bellows orders to his troops, urging them to push forward and break through the enemy lines as a nearby shaman rains fire and lightning down on the enemy army and weakens their defenses. An ogrun korune stands at the front of his troops, his shield held high as he leads the charge against a horde of blighted elves. His allies follow closely behind him, their weapons at the ready. The ogrun uses his knowledge of the battlefield to direct his troops, calling out commands and adjusting their positions as necessary. A master of defensive warfare, he uses his shield to protect his allies, deflecting enemy attacks with ease. These individuals are warlords—skilled tacticians and natural leaders in war. Their very presence on the battlefield is enough to inspire their allies and intimidate their foes.

entries.name: Big-Game Hunter
entries: You are well known for going after larger creatures and putting them down with a well-timed shot from your rifle or pistol. You certainly aren't afraid to get into the thick of things with a sword or an axe, but you feel that firearms are almost always the best tool for the job.

entries.name: Manhunter
entries: From the coldest peaks to the hottest rain forests, monsters stalk every type of land across western Immoren. It takes a special breed of monster hunter to follow these beasts through their territory and use their own methods against them.

entries.name: Conqueror
entries: Conquerors are warlords who specialize in leading their allies to victory through force and intimidation.

entries.name: Defender
entries: Defenders are warlords who specialize in protecting their allies. They are skilled at intercepting attacks, disrupting enemy movements, and inspiring their allies to stand firm in the face of danger.

entries.name: Tactician
entries: Tacticians are warlords who specialize in strategy and control. They are skilled at analyzing battlefields, directing their allies, and manipulating their enemies.

entries.name: Biochemist
